demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care facility; nursing staff present and assisting

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm bursting, the pain is unbearable, but I can't force a single drop out! (call placed by facility nursing staff on the resident's behalf)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Severe lower-tummy pain and a desperate need to pass urine but nothing will come out — it's been building all day and he's beside himself.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the last 12 hours, getting steadily worse
symptom_narrative.duration: about 12 hours and not letting up
symptom_narrative.character: a constant, intense urge to wee with agonising pain low in the tummy, but he can't pass anything voluntarily
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain he can remember — he can't keep still and is thrashing about in distress
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low down in the middle of the tummy, above the pubic bone
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps building the longer he can't go
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: the nurses have noticed small amounts of urine constantly dribbling and leaking onto the bedsheets even though he says he can't wee properly
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no full blockage like this before, but his stream has been getting weaker and more hesitant over the past year
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally gets around with a walker; right now he can't settle or stand still because of the pain

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: waterworks have been getting slower over the past year — weak stream, has to wait for it to start
past_medical_history.how_managed: takes a daily tablet from the GP for his prostate/waterworks

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: an over-the-counter antihistamine (started yesterday for an itchy rash)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility with nursing staff on hand; the antihistamine packet is on his bedside table

objective_data_offered.value: blood pressure 165/95, heart rate about 115, temperature 36.9 C, breathing 24 a minute, oxygen 97% (read out by facility nursing staff)
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: reported over the phone by nursing staff; pain-driven readings, not independently confirmed

objective_data_offered.value: he is sweaty, very restless and agitated, clutching his lower tummy and cannot keep still
small amounts of urine continuously dribbling and leaking onto the bedsheets although he says he can't wee
over-the-counter antihistamine packaging on the bedside table
